name: Pose Skeleton (Standalone)
description: UNVERIFIED — A self-contained 9-point placeholder stick-figure renderer (no instancing — bones are drawn as Script SOP polylines via a lineMAT), ready to be driven by any custom pose source. Unlike pose_skeleton_mediapipe, this recipe does NOT depend on the torinmb MediaPipe plugin: it ships a built-in Table DAT with 9 static landmark positions (head, shoulders, hips, hands, feet) feeding a Script SOP that draws a stick-figure. Drop your own CHOP/DAT/OSC source into 'landmarks' to animate it. Foundation for custom pose pipelines (Kinect, Azure, OSC, file playback).

nodes.name: landmarks
nodes.comment: Placeholder static landmarks (9 joints: head, l/r shoulder, l/r hip, l/r hand, l/r foot): name,x,y,z — replace contents to animate

nodes.name: skeleton
nodes.comment: onCook reads landmarks DAT and builds points + bone polylines

python_code.landmarks:
name	x	y	z
head	0	1.6	0
lshoulder	-0.4	1.2	0
rshoulder	0.4	1.2	0
lhip	-0.3	0.4	0
rhip	0.3	0.4	0
lhand	-0.8	0.7	0
rhand	0.8	0.7	0
lfoot	-0.3	-0.8	0
rfoot	0.3	-0.8	0
python_code.skel_cb:
BONES = [[0,1],[0,2],[1,2],[1,3],[2,4],[3,4],[1,5],[2,6],[3,7],[4,8]]

def onCook(scriptOp):
    scriptOp.clear()
    src = op('../landmarks')
    if src is None or src.numRows < 2:
        return
    pts = []
    for r in range(1, src.numRows):
        try:
            x = float(src[r, 1].val)
            y = float(src[r, 2].val)
            z = float(src[r, 3].val)
        except Exception:
            continue
        p = scriptOp.appendPoint()
        p.x = x; p.y = y; p.z = z
        pts.append(p)
    n = len(pts)
    for a, b in BONES:
        if a < n and b < n:
            poly = scriptOp.appendPoly(2, closed=False, addPoints=False)
            poly[0].point = pts[a]
            poly[1].point = pts[b]
    return

preview_description: UNVERIFIED — An orange 9-point stick-figure (drawn as bone polylines, no instancing) standing in place against black, ready to be animated by swapping the landmarks DAT for a live pose source.
